core_mechanism: uniform (chaining-style) concentration control of the supremum of a random Gaussian process indexed by a locality-/depth-constrained family of transformations, used to prove that no low-complexity member of the family can bring the objective within a fixed gap of the global optimum
brief_reason: The abstract explicitly recasts the state-preparation lower bound as 'uniform control of Gaussian processes indexed by shallow circuits,' combining a bounded-reach (light-cone) locality constraint on shallow transformations with a uniform concentration bound over the whole parameterized family to certify an unavoidable optimality gap.
